diagrams.title: OS Services Layered Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows how OS services are organized in layers from hardware to user applications
elements: OS Services (Process, Memory, File, Device Management)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how services are structured and interact with each other in an operating system.

diagrams.title: Process State Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the lifecycle of a process with different states and transitions
elements: Transitions: Admit, Dispatch, Interrupt, I/O Wait, I/O Complete, Exit
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how Process Management service controls process execution through various states.

diagrams.title: Memory Management Concepts
diagrams.description: Illustrates how OS manages memory including virtual memory and paging
elements: Page Table mapping logical to physical
diagrams.purpose: Understanding Memory Management service and how it provides illusion of larger memory.

diagrams.title: Device Management Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the layered approach to device handling in operating systems
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how Device Management service abstracts hardware complexity from applications.
